back_translation: Then after fourteen more years, I went up to the city of Jerusalem again with Barnabas, and brought Titus along too

I went up according to a revelation, and presented the gospel which I proclaim among the Gentiles to them, but I presented privately to those who are reckoned as core-leaders, because I feared that the work I am currently doing or have already done would be without result
But even Titus who was with me, who is a Greek, was not compelled to be circumcised
All this was because of false brothers who smuggled themselves in—those who sneaked in to spy on our freedom which we have in Jesus Christ, in order to lead us back into being slaves—
We did not submit to them even for a moment, so that the truth of the gospel would continue to remain with you

But from those who are reckoned as important persons—whatever they used to be before is of no importance to me, because God does not show partiality to a person's face—those core-leaders did not add any further teaching whatsoever to me

On the contrary, when they saw that I had been entrusted with the assignment to proclaim the gospel to those who are not circumcised, just as Peter had been entrusted to proclaim to those who are circumcised
for God who works in Peter for the office of apostle to those circumcised, also works in me likewise, for the Gentiles

and when James, Cephas, and John—those reckoned as pillars—knew about the grace that was bestowed upon me, they extended the right hand of co-laborer-fellowship to me and Barnabas, so that we would go to the Gentiles, and they themselves would go to those circumcised
They only requested that we remember the poor, which very matter I myself was eager to do
But when Cephas came to the city of Antioch, I opposed him to his face, because he deserved to be reproached

For before some men from the school of James arrived, Cephas used to eat together with the Gentiles. But when they arrived, he began to draw back and separate himself, because he feared those from the circumcision group
And the other Jews also went along with him in pretending too, so that even Barnabas was led along with their pretending

But when I saw that they were not walking straight according to the truth of the gospel, I therefore said to Cephas in front of everyone, 'If you yourself are a Jew, but you live like a Gentile and not like a Jew, why then do you compel the Gentiles to live like Jews?'
We are Jews by birth, not 'sinners' who come from the Gentiles

knowing that a person is not declared righteous by conduct according to the Law, but by faith in Jesus Christ; therefore we ourselves believed in Jesus Christ, in order to be declared righteous by faith in Christ, not by conduct according to the Law, because no flesh whatsoever will be declared righteous by conduct according to the Law
But if while we are seeking to be declared righteous in Christ, we ourselves are found to be 'sinners' too, in that case is Christ then a servant of sin? There is no way it could be so
For if I rebuild what I have already torn down, I prove myself to be one who violates the Law
For I have died to the Law by means of the Law, so that I might live for God. I have been crucified together with Christ

But it is I who am no longer the one living; rather, Christ is the one living in me. The life which I am currently leading in the flesh, I lead by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave Himself up for me
I do not reject the grace of God; for if righteousness comes through the Law, then Christ died without cause
